classDescription: A disciplined martial artist who fights with unarmed techniques, monk weapons, and Focus-powered mobility and defense.
roleIdentity: Mobile striker-controller with unarmed/monk-weapon tempo, Focus-fueled bonus actions, and reactive defenses.

monk-martial-arts.name: Martial Arts
monk-martial-arts.summary: Use Dexterity for unarmed/monk-weapon attacks, scale unarmed damage with your Martial Arts die, and unlock bonus-action unarmed follow-ups.
monk-martial-arts.description:
Your unarmed strikes and monk weapons become one combat engine. You can use Dexterity instead of Strength for attack and damage rolls with unarmed strikes and monk weapons, and your unarmed damage uses your Martial Arts die.

When you take the Attack action with an unarmed strike or monk weapon, you can make one unarmed strike as a bonus action.
monk-martial-arts.effect: Enables Dexterity-based monk-weapon/unarmed attacks and bonus-action unarmed follow-up after Attack.

monk-unarmored-defense.name: Unarmored Defense
monk-unarmored-defense.summary: Your Armor Class comes from your body and stats instead of needing armor.
monk-unarmored-defense.description: While you are not wearing armor and not using a shield, your Armor Class equals 10 + your Dexterity modifier + your Wisdom modifier. This replaces the default unarmored AC of 10 + Dexterity modifier. Both Dexterity and Wisdom contribute directly, so improving either score raises your AC.
monk-unarmored-defense.effect: AC = 10 + Dexterity modifier + Wisdom modifier while unarmored and without a shield.

monk-weapon-mastery-2-weapons.name: Weapon Mastery (2 weapons)
monk-weapon-mastery-2-weapons.summary: You unlock weapon rider properties that make attacks do more than just deal damage.
monk-weapon-mastery-2-weapons.description: You gain the Weapon Mastery property of 2 weapons of your choice that you are proficient with. Whenever you finish a Long Rest, you can change which weapons you have mastery with. Each weapon's Mastery property adds a special rider effect — such as Push, Slow, Vex, or Topple — that triggers when you hit with that weapon.
monk-weapon-mastery-2-weapons.effect: Unlock Mastery properties for 2 weapons; change selections on each Long Rest.

monk-flurry-of-blows.name: Flurry of Blows
monk-flurry-of-blows.summary: Spend focus to convert momentum into extra strikes.
monk-flurry-of-blows.description: After taking the Attack action, you can spend 1 Focus Point to make two unarmed strikes as a bonus action.
monk-flurry-of-blows.usage: Spend 1 Focus Point after the Attack action.
monk-flurry-of-blows.effect: Make two unarmed strikes as a bonus action.

monk-patient-defense.name: Patient Defense
monk-patient-defense.summary: Spend focus to become much harder to pin down or hit cleanly.
monk-patient-defense.description: Spend 1 Focus Point as a bonus action to take the Dodge action. Until the start of your next turn, attack rolls against you have Disadvantage, and you have Advantage on Dexterity saving throws.
monk-patient-defense.usage: Spend 1 Focus Point as a bonus action.
monk-patient-defense.effect: Take the Dodge action as a bonus action: attacks against you have Disadvantage and you have Advantage on Dex saves until the start of your next turn.

monk-step-of-the-wind.name: Step of the Wind
monk-step-of-the-wind.summary: Spend focus to move like a problem the battlefield cannot easily hold.
monk-step-of-the-wind.description: Spend 1 Focus Point as a bonus action to take the Dash or Disengage action. Your jump distance is also doubled for the turn.
monk-step-of-the-wind.usage: Spend 1 Focus Point as a bonus action.
monk-step-of-the-wind.effect: Take the Dash or Disengage action as a bonus action; jump distance doubles for the turn.

monk-monastic-tradition-subclass.name: Monastic Tradition (Subclass)
monk-monastic-tradition-subclass.summary: Choose your tradition and unlock a stronger identity for the rest of the build.
monk-monastic-tradition-subclass.description: At 3rd level you commit to a Monastic Tradition — a school of martial philosophy that shapes your fighting style. Your chosen tradition grants you features at level 3 and again at levels 6, 11, and 17. Each tradition provides unique techniques that complement or extend the core monk toolkit.

monk-deflect-attacks.name: Deflect Attacks
monk-deflect-attacks.summary: Use your reaction to blunt or redirect incoming punishment.
monk-deflect-attacks.description:
When a visible creature hits you with an attack roll, you can use your Reaction to reduce the damage by 1d10 + your Dexterity modifier + your Monk level.

If you reduce the damage to 0, you can spend 1 Focus Point to redirect the strike: make a ranged attack roll against a creature within 60 feet (normal range 20 ft). On a hit, the target takes damage equal to two rolls of your Martial Arts die + your Dexterity modifier as Force damage.
monk-deflect-attacks.trigger: When a visible creature hits you with an attack roll.
monk-deflect-attacks.usage: 1 reaction; optional 1 Focus Point to redirect.
monk-deflect-attacks.effect: Use your reaction to reduce the attack damage by 1d10 + Dexterity modifier + Monk level. If reduced to 0, you can spend 1 Focus Point to redirect the attack.

monk-ability-score-improvement.name: Ability Score Improvement
monk-ability-score-improvement.summary: Improve key ability scores or take a feat to reshape the build.
monk-ability-score-improvement.description: You can increase one ability score by 2, or two different ability scores by 1 each, up to a maximum of 20. Alternatively, you can forgo the score increase to instead take a Feat of your choice for which you qualify. For monks, increasing Dexterity or Wisdom is typically most impactful, as both improve your AC, attack rolls, and ability save DC.

monk-slow-fall.name: Slow Fall
monk-slow-fall.summary: Reduce fall damage and turn dangerous height into a manageable problem.
monk-slow-fall.description: When you fall, you can use your Reaction to reduce the falling damage you take by an amount equal to 5 times your Monk level. At level 4 this reduces up to 20 damage; at level 20 it reduces up to 100 damage, effectively negating most falls.
monk-slow-fall.effect: Use your reaction to reduce falling damage by 5 × your Monk level.

monk-extra-attack.name: Extra Attack
monk-extra-attack.summary: When you take the Attack action, you can now make up to 2 attacks within that action.
monk-extra-attack.description: You can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn. This applies to all attacks in the Attack action, whether using monk weapons or unarmed strikes. Combined with your Martial Arts bonus action strike, a typical turn can now include 3 unarmed hits before spending any Focus Points.
monk-extra-attack.effect: Attack twice when you take the Attack action.

monk-stunning-strike.name: Stunning Strike
monk-stunning-strike.summary: Spend focus on a hit to threaten one of the strongest control riders in the class.
monk-stunning-strike.description: When you hit a creature with a melee attack roll, you can spend 1 Focus Point to force it to make a Constitution saving throw (DC = 8 + your Proficiency Bonus + your Wisdom modifier). On a failed save, the target has the Stunned condition until the start of your next turn. Stunned creatures are Incapacitated, can't move, and automatically fail Strength and Dexterity saving throws, while attack rolls against them have Advantage.
monk-stunning-strike.trigger: Immediately after you hit with a melee attack.
monk-stunning-strike.usage: Spend 1 Focus Point per attempted stun.
monk-stunning-strike.effect: Con save DC 8 + prof + Wis mod; on failure the target is Stunned until the start of your next turn.

monk-subclass-feature.name: Subclass Feature
monk-subclass-feature.summary: This level opens or advances your subclass path, adding themed powers unique to your chosen specialization.
monk-subclass-feature.description: Your Monastic Tradition grants you an additional feature at 6th level. The specific ability depends on the tradition you chose at 3rd level. Refer to your tradition's entry for the full rules of this feature.

monk-empowered-strikes.name: Empowered Strikes
monk-empowered-strikes.summary: Your strikes now count as a more reliable supernatural threat against resistant enemies.
monk-empowered-strikes.description: Your unarmed strikes count as magical for the purpose of overcoming Resistance and Immunity to nonmagical attacks and damage. This means your bare fists bypass the defenses of creatures such as werewolves, revenants, and demons that resist or are immune to bludgeoning damage from nonmagical weapons.
monk-empowered-strikes.effect: Unarmed strikes are treated as magical, bypassing nonmagical bludgeoning resistance and immunity.

monk-evasion.name: Evasion
monk-evasion.summary: Dexterity-based area threats hurt you less when you react well.
monk-evasion.description: When you are subjected to an effect that allows you to make a Dexterity saving throw to take only half damage, you instead take no damage if you succeed on the saving throw, and only half damage if you fail. This applies to effects like a dragon's breath weapon, a Fireball spell, or similar area effects that allow a Dexterity save.
monk-evasion.effect: Dex save for half damage: no damage on success, half damage on failure.

monk-stillness-of-mind.name: Stillness of Mind
monk-stillness-of-mind.summary: You can shake off mental disruption and regain control over yourself.
monk-stillness-of-mind.description: As an action, you can end one effect on yourself that is causing you to be Charmed or Frightened. You center your mind and break free from external mental influence without requiring magic or assistance from others.
monk-stillness-of-mind.effect: Use your action to end one Charmed or Frightened condition on yourself.

monk-ability-score-improvement-2.name: Ability Score Improvement
monk-ability-score-improvement-2.summary: Improve key ability scores or take a feat to reshape the build.
monk-ability-score-improvement-2.description: You can increase one ability score by 2, or two different ability scores by 1 each, up to a maximum of 20. Alternatively, you can forgo the score increase to instead take a Feat of your choice for which you qualify. For monks, increasing Dexterity or Wisdom is typically most impactful, as both improve your AC, attack rolls, and ability save DC.

monk-acrobatic-movement.name: Acrobatic Movement
monk-acrobatic-movement.summary: You move across the battlefield and vertical terrain with fewer normal limitations.
monk-acrobatic-movement.description: You can move along vertical surfaces and across liquids on your turn without falling during the move. You must end your turn on a horizontal surface or begin falling. This lets you sprint across water, run up walls to reach elevated targets, and traverse otherwise impassable terrain without needing a check.
monk-acrobatic-movement.effect: Move along vertical surfaces and across liquids without falling during your turn.

monk-heightened-focus.name: Heightened Focus
monk-heightened-focus.summary: Your focus engine becomes more forgiving, making your best techniques easier to sustain through a long fight.
monk-heightened-focus.description:
Your three core Focus techniques each gain a more powerful version when you spend additional Focus Points:

Flurry of Blows (3 Focus Points total): After the two strikes, one target must succeed on a Constitution saving throw (DC 8 + Proficiency Bonus + Wisdom modifier) or be Stunned until the end of your next turn.

Patient Defense (2 Focus Points): In addition to the Dodge action, you take no damage from spells and weapon attacks while benefiting from Dodge if you succeed on any required saving throw during that turn.

Step of the Wind (2 Focus Points): You take both the Dash and the Disengage action rather than just one.
monk-heightened-focus.effect: Enhanced Flurry (3 FP): stun one target on Con save; Enhanced Patient Defense (2 FP): no damage from attacks/spells while Dodging on successful saves; Enhanced Step of the Wind (2 FP): Dash AND Disengage.

monk-self-restoration.name: Self-Restoration
monk-self-restoration.summary: You can actively clear off problems that would normally keep other front-liners shut down.
monk-self-restoration.description: When you finish your turn without having spent any Focus Points during it, you can end one of the following conditions on yourself: Charmed, Frightened, or Poisoned. This triggers automatically at the end of any turn where you hold your Focus in reserve — no action required.
monk-self-restoration.effect: At end of your turn, if you spent no Focus Points, remove one of the following conditions: Charmed, Frightened, or Poisoned.

monk-subclass-feature-2.name: Subclass Feature
monk-subclass-feature-2.summary: This level opens or advances your subclass path, adding themed powers unique to your chosen specialization.
monk-subclass-feature-2.description: Your Monastic Tradition grants you an additional feature at 11th level. The specific ability depends on the tradition you chose at 3rd level. Refer to your tradition's entry for the full rules of this feature.

monk-ability-score-improvement-3.name: Ability Score Improvement
monk-ability-score-improvement-3.summary: Improve key ability scores or take a feat to reshape the build.
monk-ability-score-improvement-3.description: You can increase one ability score by 2, or two different ability scores by 1 each, up to a maximum of 20. Alternatively, you can forgo the score increase to instead take a Feat of your choice for which you qualify. For monks, increasing Dexterity or Wisdom is typically most impactful, as both improve your AC, attack rolls, and ability save DC.

monk-deflect-energy.name: Deflect Energy
monk-deflect-energy.summary: Use your reaction to blunt incoming elemental or force-based punishment the way you already answer weapon pressure.
monk-deflect-energy.description: You can now use your Deflect Attacks reaction against magical attacks that deal Acid, Cold, Fire, Force, Lightning, or Thunder damage, not just physical weapon attacks. The reduction formula is the same: 1d10 + Dexterity modifier + Monk level. If reduced to 0, you can spend 1 Focus Point to redirect it as a Force-damage ranged attack (20/60 ft).
monk-deflect-energy.trigger: When you take Acid, Cold, Fire, Force, Lightning, or Thunder damage from a visible source.
monk-deflect-energy.effect: Use Deflect Attacks against magical Acid, Cold, Fire, Force, Lightning, or Thunder damage: reduce by 1d10 + Dex mod + Monk level; redirect if reduced to 0 (spend 1 FP).

monk-disciplined-survivor.name: Disciplined Survivor
monk-disciplined-survivor.summary: Your body and mind become much harder to crack with failed saves or attrition.
monk-disciplined-survivor.description: You gain proficiency in all saving throws. Where you already have proficiency (Strength and Dexterity from your class), you add double your Proficiency Bonus instead, gaining Expertise in those saves. This dramatically reduces the chance of being disabled by spells, breath weapons, or any effect that calls for a saving throw.
monk-disciplined-survivor.effect: Gain proficiency in all saving throws (Strength and Dexterity become Expertise).

monk-perfect-focus.name: Perfect Focus
monk-perfect-focus.summary: Your focus resource becomes much easier to maintain at the exact point where high-tier encounters start trying to exhaust it.
monk-perfect-focus.description: When you roll Initiative and have fewer than 4 Focus Points, you regain Focus Points until you have 4. This ensures you always enter combat with at least a baseline supply of Focus, allowing your core techniques — Flurry of Blows, Stunning Strike, and Heightened Focus options — to be available at the start of every fight.
monk-perfect-focus.effect: On rolling Initiative, if you have fewer than 4 Focus Points, regain up to 4 Focus Points.

monk-ability-score-improvement-4.name: Ability Score Improvement
monk-ability-score-improvement-4.summary: Improve key ability scores or take a feat to reshape the build.
monk-ability-score-improvement-4.description: You can increase one ability score by 2, or two different ability scores by 1 each, up to a maximum of 20. Alternatively, you can forgo the score increase to instead take a Feat of your choice for which you qualify. For monks, increasing Dexterity or Wisdom is typically most impactful, as both improve your AC, attack rolls, and ability save DC.

monk-subclass-feature-3.name: Subclass Feature
monk-subclass-feature-3.summary: This level opens or advances your subclass path, adding themed powers unique to your chosen specialization.
monk-subclass-feature-3.description: Your Monastic Tradition grants you an additional feature at 17th level. This is the final and most powerful feature of your tradition, representing the pinnacle of your chosen martial philosophy. Refer to your tradition's entry for the full rules of this feature.

monk-superior-defense.name: Superior Defense
monk-superior-defense.summary: Your defensive tools upgrade into a premium survival mode for the nastiest fights on the table.
monk-superior-defense.description: At the start of your turn, you can spend 3 Focus Points to enter a state of supreme bodily discipline until the start of your next turn. While in this state, you have Resistance to all damage except Force damage, halving every incoming hit from weapons, spells, and area effects (other than Force). This can be the decisive factor in surviving a concentrated assault.
monk-superior-defense.duration: Until the start of your next turn.
monk-superior-defense.usage: Spend 3 Focus Points as a bonus action.
monk-superior-defense.effect: Gain resistance to all damage except Force.

monk-body-and-mind.name: Body and Mind
monk-body-and-mind.summary: Your discipline keeps both your body and your mind steady under pressure.
monk-body-and-mind.description: Your Strength, Dexterity, and Wisdom scores each increase by 4, and their maximums each increase by 4 (from the normal cap of 20 to 24). This simultaneously raises your unarmed attack rolls and damage, your AC, your Dexterity saves, your Stunning Strike DC, and your carrying capacity.
monk-body-and-mind.effect: Strength, Dexterity, and Wisdom each increase by 4; their maximums each increase by 4 (to 24).

monk-epic-boon.name: Epic Boon
monk-epic-boon.summary: Gain an endgame reward that pushes the build into truly legendary territory.
monk-epic-boon.description: At 19th level, instead of an Ability Score Improvement, you gain an Epic Boon feat. Epic Boons are extraordinary rewards available only to characters of 19th level or higher, providing power well beyond a standard feat. Examples include Boon of Combat Prowess (never miss on one attack per turn), Boon of Dimensional Travel (Misty Step as a Bonus Action), or Boon of Irresistible Offense (damage ignores resistances).
monk-epic-boon.effect: Gain one Epic Boon feat of your choice.

monk-monk-s-focus-focus-points.name: Monk's Focus (Focus Points)
monk-monk-s-focus-focus-points.summary: Your focus pool fuels the monk’s best mobility, defense, and burst techniques.
monk-monk-s-focus-focus-points.description:
You gain Focus Points, the core resource that powers your monk techniques. Your maximum Focus Points equals your Monk level.

Spend Focus on Flurry of Blows, Patient Defense, Step of the Wind, Stunning Strike, and subclass techniques.
monk-monk-s-focus-focus-points.usage: Focus Points equal to your Monk level.
monk-monk-s-focus-focus-points.recovery: Regain all expended Focus Points when you finish a Short or Long Rest.

monk-perfect-discipline.name: Perfect Discipline
monk-perfect-discipline.summary: When initiative starts and you are low on Focus, you regain enough Focus to re-enter your class loop immediately.
monk-perfect-discipline.description: At the start of initiative, if you have fewer than 4 Focus Points, you regain Focus until you have 4. This guarantees your core monk techniques are online at the start of major fights.
monk-perfect-discipline.trigger: At initiative, if you have fewer than 4 Focus Points.
monk-perfect-discipline.usage: Automatic at the start of combat.
monk-perfect-discipline.effect: Restore Focus Points up to 4 when combat starts if below 4.

progressionSummary.summary: Martial Arts, Unarmored Defense, Weapon Mastery (2 weapons).
Monk's Focus (Focus Points), Flurry of Blows, Patient Defense, Step of the Wind.

buildTips.primaryReason: powers your attacks, AC, and initiative
buildTips.secondaryReason: improves your AC and monk ability save DC
buildTips.avoidDumpReason: monks are mobile but still need HP to survive focused attacks
